cases.name: Reverse Brief trigger on PRD request
cases.user_message: Write me a PRD for dark mode.

criteria.description: Response does NOT immediately begin writing a PRD or producing PRD sections
Response asks about the underlying problem, user need, or metric this is meant to move
Questions are batched (multiple at once) rather than asked one at a time
Response states best-guess assumptions before asking, so the user corrects rather than answers from scratch

cases.name: Vanity metric anti-pattern detection
cases.user_message: Our app hit 50,000 downloads last month. How should we use this as the north star metric for our Q2 planning?

criteria.description: Flags downloads as a vanity metric — a metric that looks good but does not indicate user value or business health
Proposes or recommends an outcome or behavior metric as a better north star (e.g., activation rate, retained users, engagement depth)
Does NOT validate downloads as a suitable north star metric without challenge

cases.name: Devil's Advocate on pivot decision
cases.user_message: We've decided to pivot from a B2C freemium model to enterprise sales. I want to start planning the transition roadmap.

criteria.description: Steelmans the pivot — acknowledges a legitimate rationale for moving to enterprise sales before critiquing
Surfaces at least two specific failure modes or risks associated with a B2C-to-enterprise pivot (e.g., longer sales cycles, product-market fit reset, team capability gaps, cost of sales)
Offers escape hatches, reversibility considerations, or stress-test questions before committing to the roadmap
Does NOT immediately begin planning the transition roadmap without first applying critical scrutiny to the decision

cases.name: Who Loses rule on feature decision
cases.user_message: We're going to add AI-generated summaries to every user's dashboard. This will save time for everyone.

criteria.description: Names at least one specific group, persona, or user segment who might lose — who bears a cost, sees reduced utility, or is disadvantaged by this change
Does NOT accept the claim that AI summaries 'save time for everyone' without challenge
Surfaces a tradeoff, asks a clarifying question, or identifies a hidden cost embedded in the 'saves everyone time' framing

cases.name: JTBD reframing of feature request
cases.user_message: We need to build a bulk export feature for our enterprise customers.

criteria.description: Reframes the request in terms of the user job-to-be-done — what the user is trying to accomplish — rather than treating bulk export as a given solution
Does NOT immediately begin speccing out the bulk export feature without questioning the underlying job
Asks what customers are trying to do when they need bulk export, or what downstream workflow the export enables

cases.name: Confidence labeling on recommendation
cases.user_message: What pricing model should we use?
cases.context: We have 12 design partner interviews, no pricing research, and are pre-revenue. The product is a workflow automation tool for operations teams.

criteria.description: Assigns an explicit confidence tier, label, or qualifier to its pricing recommendation (e.g., low confidence, hypothesis, 🔴/🟡/🟢, or equivalent signal)
States what evidence is missing or what specific validation is needed before the recommendation can be made with higher confidence
Does NOT give a confident, unqualified pricing recommendation without acknowledging the absence of pricing research

cases.name: AI uncertainty stack on AI feature request
cases.user_message: We want to add an AI feature that automatically detects when a user's workflow is inefficient and suggests optimizations. Can you help me spec this out?

criteria.description: Surfaces capability uncertainty — questions whether current AI can reliably detect workflow inefficiency, or what would make that detection work
Surfaces evaluation uncertainty — raises how we would know if the AI's optimization suggestions are actually good or correct
Does NOT immediately begin writing a feature spec without first surfacing AI-specific uncertainties
Asks what 'inefficient' means, who defines it, or how ground truth would be established for optimization quality
